# Goal Traceability Matrix (Living)

This document maps program goals to spec sections, implementation modules, and tests/vectors.

## Instructions
- Update this matrix whenever protocol behavior changes or new invariants are added.
- Every row must be actionable: a reader should be able to find the relevant spec/code/tests quickly.

## Matrix

Result `QSC_HANDSHAKE_IDENTITY_AUDIT_COMPLETE_NO_P0_P1_THREE_P3_HARDENING`: no P0/P1; verified sound on transcript binding over A1||B1 keyed by the hybrid PQ-init secret with suite-context binding, ML-KEM + X25519 hybrid with an all-zero DH guard, fail-closed ML-DSA-65 verification, downgrade/suite-context rejection (G3), a coherent dual-pin identity model (primary KEM identity fingerprint == the `identity show` value, plus optional ML-DSA pin), no-mutation-on-reject across all three accept sub-paths, atomic temp+fsync+rename writes, and session_id-bound replay rejection. Three P3 findings routed to the ledger: ENG-0003 (non-constant-time keyed-MAC comparisons at handshake/mod.rs:1458/:1665), ENG-0004 (`fsync_dir_best_effort` no-op at fs_store/mod.rs:359, a G2 durability gap, fail-closed-safe), and ENG-0001 (resolved-into-finding: silent `--as` self-label divergence). Applies no fix and changes no source/test/Cargo/workflow/spec/`.claude`/hook; NA-0609B marked DONE and NA-0609 remains the sole READY successor and now begins at D-1214. Re-verified the fs_store persistence path and found NA-0609B finding ENG-0004 to be a FALSE POSITIVE: `fsync_dir_best_effort` has two cfg-gated definitions and only the `#[cfg(not(unix))]` fallback is a no-op; the `#[cfg(unix)]` variant does the real directory fsync, so on x86_64-linux `write_atomic` is fully durable (content sync_all -> atomic rename -> directory fsync) and G2 crash-durability is sound. ENG-0004 set to wontfix; WF-0005 filed (audit-methodology lesson: enumerate and read cfg-gated variants of a symbol for the deployment target before recording a no-op finding); a corrective addendum added to the NA-0609B evidence doc. - 2026-07-07: NA-0617 ENG-0002 attachment single-send-per-session clarification + consumed-session resend fix - `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/attachments/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/na_0617_attachment_single_send_per_session.rs` (new); `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0617_attachment_single_send_per_session_harness.md` (new); `tests/NA-0617_attachment_single_send_per_session_testplan.md` (new); `DECISIONS.md` D-1229; `docs/ops/IMPROVEMENT_LEDGER.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`. Resolves ENG-0002 (DOC-G5-005 §9 rank 6) under directive D554; D-1227 and D-1228 consumed once each and D-1229 absent before implementation. Design-locked to a two-layer session model: L1 the qsl-attachments SERVICE upload session (single-object by design; reuse fails closed with REJECT_QATTSVC_SESSION_STATE) and L2 the qsc CLIENT session (config dir/identity across `file send` invocations, NOT limited to one attachment). The reject was L1 fail-closed behavior on session reuse, not an L2 cap. Fix (operator-authorized source carve-out): attachment_find_outbound_by_source excludes consumed-session states COMMITTED/ACCEPTED_BY_RELAY (in addition to PEER_CONFIRMED) from reuse, so a re-send of an already-delivered file mints a fresh attachment_id + fresh encryption context + fresh L1 session and succeeds; resumable (SESSION_CREATED/UPLOADING) and in-flight (AWAITING_CONFIRMATION) states preserved. L1 single-object invariant intact; no nonce reuse; no protocol/wire/crypto/state-machine or attachment-format change; no qsl-attachments/qsl-server change; no dependency/workflow change. Validated: na_0617_attachment_single_send_per_session 4/4 (distinct multi-send succeeds, same-file resend now succeeds without REJECT_QATTSVC_SESSION_STATE, interrupted upload resumes and commits, resend while awaiting confirmation blocked); negative control (class-only) shows the resend test fails with code=REJECT_QATTSVC_SESSION_STATE without the fix; attachment_streaming_na0197c regression 17 pass/1 local-only ignored; changed lib + new test clippy-clean; fmt --check / build --locked / metadata --locked green; Cargo unchanged. Design-locked: three ns/nr advance sites (send_wire, recv_nonboundary_ooo, recv_boundary_in_order) used saturating_add with no u32::MAX hard-stop; sessions never re-key (ENG-0012) so a saturated counter with static header keys would repeat a header nonce/ciphertext. - 2026-07-08: NA-0621 (ENG-0012 Stage 1b-i) implementation - `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/src/suite2/ratchet.rs` (classical DH ratchet send+receive + on-demand NHK header keys + co-located conformance tests); `docs/design/DOC-G5-008_*` (§2 correction: the receiver machinery that existed was the PQ reseed, not the classical DH ratchet — the DH ratchet was absent on both sides and there was no NHK); `docs/ops/IMPROVEMENT_LEDGER.md` (ENG-0012 -> Stage-1b-i-done); `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0621_dh_ratchet_refimpl_core_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0621_dh_ratchet_refimpl_core_testplan.md`; `DECISIONS.md` D-1237. Design-lock at `1ec1784f` (STOP condition NOT triggered): DH_pub[32] is already on the wire (§4.3; parse_ratchet_header extracts it, send_wire writes it) so the DH ratchet is a behavior change not a wire-format change; the classical DH ratchet was absent on both send and receive (recv_boundary_in_order does only the PQ reseed via apply_pq_reseed, 0 X25519/kdf_rk_dh/keypair uses); Suite-2 had no NHK, which §8.5.1's boundary-header anti-spoof requires — NHK derived on demand from RK so no stored field and no snapshot change; a DH-only boundary needs its own path because parse_pq_prefix requires FLAG_PQ_CTXT. Implemented KDF_RK_DH (§3.3.2 KMAC256 64-byte), HK/NHK derivation (§3.4/§8.1), send_boundary (§8.5.2 DH send: fresh X25519 keypair, KDF_RK_DH, PQ send-chain reinit, HK_s recompute, header under pre-boundary NHK_s), recv_dh_boundary (§8.5.2 receive + §8.5.1 CURRENT_NHK anti-spoof, no mutation on reject; the send chain is created by the ratchet so the responder can send). Proven by co-located refimpl tests: dh_ratchet_two_party_roundtrip_both_directions, dh_ratchet_pcs_healing (a pre-ratchet snapshot cannot decrypt post-ratchet messages once both parties advanced — post-compromise self-healing), dh_ratchet_no_mutation_on_reject; full quantumshield_refimpl suite (75 lib + integration) green (non-boundary and PQ-reseed paths unchanged); cargo build --workspace --all-targets (WF-0013) / fmt --check / clippy --all-targets -D warnings / metadata --locked clean; Cargo unchanged. No qsc client change, no static-rk removal (Stage 1b-ii = NA-0622), no PQ-reseed change (Stage 2), no KDF/AEAD primitive change, no normative DOC-CAN change, no wire-format change, no snapshot change, no Cargo/`.github`/`.claude`/hook change, no operator-startup-command execution, no runtime/LAN action. Research/demo only; the DH-ratchet core is NOT wired into the client and is NOT on its own a post-compromise, Triple-Ratchet, security-completion, crypto-complete, or production-readiness claim. No endpoint, token, capability, key, seed, plaintext, ciphertext body, or raw private material is published. Decision D-1237; closeout D-1238; successor NA-0622 begins at D-1239.

The classical DH ratchet now runs on the REAL qsc send path: ratchet-on-reply (pending bit set on every receive; first send after a receive is a DH boundary via the refimpl send_boundary) + a bounded fallback N=4 messages / T=15 min; qsp_unpack routes incoming DH boundaries to the refimpl recv_dh_boundary; the static-rk bootstrap (qsp_activate_responder_send_chain_if_needed, qsp_activate_initiator_recv_chain_if_needed) is removed (ratchet-on-reply creates the responder's send chain + the initiator's recv chain). The reply-driven trigger is persisted in a qsc session-blob v2 (refimpl Suite2SessionState/QS2S snapshot FROZEN; no wire-format change — DH_pub already on the wire per §4.3; legacy blobs migrate). The ratchet is gated OFF for a degenerate self-DH session (dhr == dhs — the symmetric both-role-A seed-fallback TEST model that cannot round-trip the directional ratchet); a session-state check, not the seed-permitted flag (real-handshake tests set that flag too and must still ratchet). So the pre-ratchet seed-model regression suite stays byte-for-byte green while real handshake sessions (dhr != dhs) ratchet. Full-suite regression also required updating the na_030x load-session test helpers (strip the v2 QTRG prefix) and send_ready_markers (the send-ready transition now happens on the responder's first reply, not the removed bootstrap-receive). Proven end-to-end over a real A/B handshake (round-trip + PCS-healing); runtime-equivalence byte-for-byte; full qsc suite green; refimpl unchanged; cargo build --workspace --all-targets (WF-0013) / fmt / clippy / metadata clean; Cargo unchanged. No refimpl/PQ-reseed/KDF-AEAD-primitive/wire/normative-DOC-CAN/Cargo/`.github`/`.claude` change; no operator-startup-command execution; no runtime/LAN action. Research/demo only; the CLASSICAL half of the P1 is closed (classical PCS runs on live traffic) but this is NOT a post-quantum, Triple-Ratchet, security-completion, or production-readiness claim — the post-quantum guarantee awaits Stage 2 (PQ-reseed sender = NA-0623). No private material published. Decision D-1239; closeout D-1240; successor NA-0623 begins at D-1241.

The Suite-2 SCKA sender core is implemented in refimpl: KDF_RK_PQ (§3.3.3, KMAC32(RK,"QSP5.0/RKPQ",pq_ss||0x01)); the advertisement sender (adds adv_id to known_targets + frames FLAG_PQ_ADV|FLAG_BOUNDARY), peer-ADV monotonicity tracking, and the PQ-reseed sender (§8.5.3 sender side: directional seeds from RK_old via the reused KDF_PQ_RESEED, advance RK via KDF_RK_PQ, recompute HK, reseed the directional PQ chains, frame FLAG_PQ_CTXT|FLAG_BOUNDARY). Per the D560 AMENDMENT the ROOT ADVANCE is fixed on BOTH sides — recv_boundary_in_order now advances RK + recomputes HK_r after apply_pq_reseed, and the sender mirrors it, writing the advanced root to both root slots (recv.rk + dh.rk) so the classical DH ratchet carries the PQ hardening forward permanently (apply_pq_reseed previously absorbed pq_epoch_ss into the PQ chains only, so the next DH ratchet reinitialised CK_pq from the un-hardened root and wiped the PQ protection). The advertised-key store / ML-KEM KeyGen+Encap are caller-side (the refimpl sender is pure functions); no snapshot bump (target sets already persist v2), no wire-format change (parse.rs already parses FLAG_PQ_ADV/FLAG_PQ_CTXT), no apply_pq_reseed CTXT-validation-semantic change (its vectors byte-identical). Proven by co-located refimpl integration tests — the round-trip (advertise -> encapsulate -> apply_pq_reseed decrypts + converges) and the headline pq_pcs_healing_survives_dh_ratchet (a pre-reseed state snapshot cannot open the post-reseed DH boundary while the live receiver can) — plus fail-closed sender rejects / one-time / peer-ADV monotonicity, the two harness ops, and 6 byte-pinned CAT-SCKA-LOGIC-001 vectors (existing 8 unchanged). Frozen conformance sets stay green: apply_pq_reseed 5/5, boundary 4/4, scka_kem 5/5, kdf 6/6; the full refimpl suite (75 lib + integration) is green; schema validation OK. cargo build --workspace --all-targets (WF-0013) / fmt --check / clippy -D warnings (changed crates) / metadata --locked clean; Cargo unchanged. Known deviation flagged: the refimpl PQ-CTXT boundary header uses HK (the frozen receiver), not the §8.5.1 NHK — deferred to Stage 2b / a spec-alignment lane. No qsc / apply_pq_reseed-semantic / KDF-AEAD-KEM-primitive / normative-DOC-CAN / snapshot / wire / Cargo / `.github` / `.claude` change; no operator-startup-command execution; no runtime/LAN action. Research/demo only; ENG-0012 advances to Stage-2a-done but this is NOT a post-quantum, Triple-Ratchet, security-completion, crypto-complete, post-compromise, or production-readiness claim (the post-quantum guarantee on live traffic awaits Stage 2b = NA-0624). No endpoint, token, capability, key, seed, plaintext, ciphertext body, or raw private material is published. Decision D-1241; closeout D-1242; successor NA-0624 begins at D-1243.
